title: Set 03 - OpenVPN, iptables, NAT, Firewalls & Security Threats
subject: NDC
topic: OpenVPN, iptables, NAT, Firewalls & Security Threats

questions.question: In OpenVPN, the option _______ is used to enable compression on the VPN Link.
questions.description: `comp-lzo` enables LZO compression in older OpenVPN versions.

questions.question: The enterprise administrator has seen an increase in the number of ping flood attacks on the web server in the DMZ. How can the administrator mitigate the attacks and prevent the web server from crashing?
options: Block ICMP Echo 8 packets on the external firewall
Block TCP 53 packets on the external firewall
Block Telnet 23 packets on the external firewall
Block ICMP Echo 15 packets on the external firewall
questions.description: Ping flood attacks use ICMP Echo Request packets, which are ICMP Type 8.

questions.question: Assault on system security that derives from an intelligent threat.
questions.description: An intelligent threat attempting to compromise system security is an attack.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes an attack that altered the contents of two critical files?
questions.description: Changing file contents violates integrity.

questions.question: Port # 137 is related with:
questions.description: TCP/UDP port 137 is used by NetBIOS Name Service.

questions.question: In iptables, the RAW table consists of ________ built-in chains:
questions.description: The RAW table has two built-in chains: PREROUTING and OUTPUT.

questions.question: On a linux machine, all of the connection tracking is done by special framework within the kernel called_____________.
questions.description: Linux connection tracking is handled by conntrack.

questions.question: In iptables, the MANGLE table consists of ________ built-in chains:
questions.description: The MANGLE table has five built-in chains.

questions.question: In iptables, the __________ table is responsible for the alteration of quality of service bits in the TCP header
questions.description: The MANGLE table is used for specialized packet alteration such as QoS/TOS/TTL marking.

questions.question: In iptables, the POSTROUTING chain in the NAT table is used for __________
questions.description: POSTROUTING in the NAT table is mainly used for source NAT.

questions.question: In iptables, the PREROUTING chain in the NAT table is used for __________
questions.description: PREROUTING in the NAT table is mainly used for destination NAT.

questions.question: A _________ allows a host on the outside to connect to a host on the inside.
questions.description: DNAT rewrites the destination address and allows outside hosts to reach internal hosts.

questions.question: _________ allows a host on the inside of the NAT, in an RFC 1918 IP address space, to initiate a connection to a host on the outside of the NAT
questions.description: SNAT rewrites the source address so inside private hosts can communicate outside.

questions.question: In a _________, the destination IP address is maintained and the source IP address is changed.
questions.description: SNAT keeps the destination address and changes the source address.

questions.question: Netfilter is a ______________Module
questions.description: Netfilter is part of the Linux kernel packet processing framework.

questions.question: The Linux kernel's network packet processing subsystem is called ________________
questions.description: Netfilter is the Linux kernel network packet processing subsystem.

questions.question: A stateful inspection firewall maintains a _________ table that is constantly updated based on the full inspection of communication flow through the firewall.
questions.description: Stateful inspection firewalls maintain a state table for active traffic flows.

questions.question: In an SPI firewall, the inspection engine operates at the __________ layer.
questions.description: Stateful packet inspection primarily evaluates Layer 3/4 packet and connection state; from these options, Network is the best answer.

questions.question: To see the TCP ephemeral port range in Windows:
options: netshow eth0 ipv4 show dynamicport tcp
netshow int ipv4 show dynamicport tcp
netsh eth0 ipv4 show dynamicport tcp
netsh int ipv4 show dynamicport tcp
questions.description: The Windows command is `netsh int ipv4 show dynamicport tcp`.

questions.question: To see the ephemeral port range in Linux:
questions.description: Linux exposes the ephemeral port range at `/proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip_local_port_range`.

questions.question: A ______________ is also known as sacrificed host.
questions.description: A bastion host is a hardened host exposed to attack and is also called a sacrificed host.

questions.question: A ______________ is a system that has been hardened to resist attack, and which is installed on a network in such a way that it is expected to potentially come under attack.
questions.description: A hardened exposed system designed to resist attack is a bastion host.

questions.question: A _____________combines a packet-filtering router with an application gateway located on the protected subnet side of the router.
questions.description: A screened host firewall combines packet filtering with an application gateway.

questions.question: Application proxy runs at layer _______
questions.description: An application proxy operates at Layer 7, the application layer.

questions.question: Which of the following command is used to view and modify the ACL of a file in Windows?
questions.description: `cacls` is the legacy Windows command for viewing and modifying ACLs.

questions.question: An ____________ is a table/database that tells an operating system which access rights each user has to a particular system object, such as a file directory or individual file.
questions.description: An ACL, or Access Control List, stores permissions for users and objects.

questions.question: ___________ is a sub-net located neither inside the internal network nor outside as part of the internet, where access is controlled, but not prevented
questions.description: A DMZ is a controlled subnet between internal and external networks.

questions.question: A ____________ is a device or set of devices configured to permit, deny, encrypt, decrypt, or proxy all computer traffic between different security domains based upon a set of rules and other criteria.
questions.description: A firewall controls traffic between security domains according to rules.

questions.question: A _________________ is a computer connected to the Internet that has been compromised by a hacker, computer virus or trojan horse and can be used to perform malicious tasks of one sort or another under remote direction.
questions.description: A compromised remotely controlled computer is a zombie.

questions.question: Cold boot attack is an attack on ____________
questions.description: Cold boot attacks recover sensitive cryptographic material from RAM.

questions.question: The act of locating and possibly exploiting connections to wireless LANs while driving around a city or elsewhere is known as:
questions.description: Searching for wireless networks while moving is wardriving.

questions.question: Practice of sifting through commercial or residential trash to find items that have been discarded by their owners, but which may be useful to the attacker is known as:
questions.description: Searching discarded trash for useful information is dumpster diving.

questions.question: ___________ is the criminal practice of using social engineering and Voice over IP (VoIP) to gain access to private personal and financial information from the public for the purpose of financial reward.
questions.description: Voice-based phishing over phone or VoIP is vishing.

questions.question: The additional element used in a DRDoS attack is known as ________
questions.description: Distributed reflected DoS attacks use reflectors.

questions.question: Flooding the bandwidth of the victim's network so that he cannot use the internet or other services is an example of ____________
questions.description: Flooding bandwidth to deny service is a DoS attack.

questions.question: SSL is replaced by ________
questions.description: TLS is the successor to SSL.

questions.question: Any potential danger to information or systems is known as
questions.description: A threat is a potential danger to information or systems.

questions.question: Weakness in a mechanism that can threaten the Confidentiality, Integrity, or Availability of an asset is known as _______
questions.description: A vulnerability is a weakness that can be exploited.

questions.question: The concept of protecting data from modification or deletion by unauthorized parties, and ensuring that when authorized people make changes that should not have been made, the damage can be undone is known as ______
questions.description: Integrity protects data from unauthorized or improper modification.

questions.question: Which of the following is an IPSec Mode?
questions.description: IPsec supports Transport Mode and Tunnel Mode; Transport Mode is listed here.
